rulings.Case Title: HOWARD I. MAIBACH VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Determination Of Amount Of Award Of Attorney?S Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 23, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER HOWARD MAIBACH'S Motion for Determination of Amount of Award of Attorney's Fees and Costs. Petitioner Howard I. Maibach's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is continued for hearing to April 6, 2026 at 9 a.m. Maibach is ordered to lodge with the court an electronic spreadsheet version of Exhibits 52 and 53 to the Lan Vu Declaration filed on February 5, 2026, by March 30, 2026, with a service copy to the Regents. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT WILLIAMS VS. AUTO-CHLOR SYSTEM OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions For Violation Of Court Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 23, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF VINCENT WILLIAMS' Motion to Compel Compliance With Court Order and for Sanctions for Violation of Court Order. Plaintiff's motion to compel compliance is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff seeks production of the time records for the employees with last names starting with M-Z in response to request for production 25. On 2/24/26, after plaintiff filed this motion, defendant produced the documents. (Horowitz Decl., par. 18; Ex. 9.) The motion is therefore denied as moot as to the additional production of documents. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against defendant is granted. Plaintiff sufficiently explained what documents were missing but defendant incorrectly insisted that the documents were already produced. (Lofton Decl., pars. 11-12; Ex. D; Second Lofton Decl., par. 3; Ex. B.) Defendant's position lacked substantial justification, and defendant shall pay $6,000 as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: INFLECT INC. VS. LUMEN TECHNOLOGIES GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Cross-Complainant Centurylink Communications, Llc Dba Lumen Technology Services Group'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 23, 2026, line 5. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Centurylink Communications, LLC's Motion For Protective Order. Centurylink Communications, LLC d/b/a Lumen's motion for protective order is granted. The deposition of Dan Albert, executive director of Solana Foundation, will take place after all parties to the consolidated action have completed production and review of documents relevant to the deposition that are the subject of existing (i.e. already-served) requests for production. The parties will meet and confer concerning that further date and any party may seek a further order if there is disagreement about the appropriate time for the Albert deposition. The court finds good cause; ordering depositions after production is more efficient and prevents burdensome and duplicative depositions. The court does not award sanctions, finding that both parties' positions had substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 23, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA's Motion for an Order Deeming the Truth of the Matters Specified in Plaintiffs Request for Admissions as Admitted. The unopposed motion for a deemed-admitted order is granted. Defendant Tung Wai Chan has not responded to the requests for admission served by plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A. and attached as Exhibit 1 to the Lopez Declaration. The matters specified in those RFAs are deemed admitted by defendant. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Wells Fargo does not seek sanctions, but they are mandatory here. (Id., subd. (c).) Defendant shall pay $50 to plaintiff within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA WESTBROOK VS. PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY, AN OHIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Stay Of The Proceedings Pending Appeal
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 23, 2026, line 9. DEFENDANTS' Motion for Stay of the Proceedings Pending Appeal. Defendants Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, Daniel Shevtsov, Henry Gregory Nelch, Mahmour A. Faldi, and Randolph S. Hicks' motion for stay of the proceedings pending appeal is granted. The case is stayed for all purposes until resolution of the pending appeal in Alsaedi et al. v. Westbrook et al., First District Court of Appeal, Case No. A170425. This case arises out of a personal injury lawsuit that resulted in a large judgment against plaintiff Joshua Westbook. The judgment is currently on appeal. "[A] court ordinarily has inherent power, in its discretion, to stay proceedings when such a stay will accommodate the ends of justice." (St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Company v. AmerisourceBergen Corporation (2022) 80 Cal.App.5th 1, 6-7 [citation simplified].) A stay in this case is appropriate, as the disposition of the appeal directly impacts a determination of causation and damages and may prevent simultaneous and duplicative litigation. (See Beal Bank, SSB v. Arter & Hadden, LLP (2007) 42 Cal.4th 503, 512-514; Jordache Enterprises, Inc. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal. 4th 739, 750, 758.) The court is unpersuaded by Plaintiff's argument that the stay will substantially prejudice him through a loss of evidence. There is no realistic prospect of lost evidence here. The court takes judicial notice sua sponte of the docket in Case No. A170425 and notes that the appeal is fully briefed. The court observes that Westbrook's FAC changed the case caption, renaming "Progressive Casualty Insurance Company" (from the original complaint) as "Drive New Jersey Insurance Company" (in the FAC). No party objected, but plaintiff is cautioned that a court order is required to change the case caption, and a filing that contains new parties without court order may be stricken. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 473, subd. (a)(1) [court may allow amendment to correct a mistake in the name of a party].) In future filings, the parties should include the original party names but may include the corrected party name as well (e.g. "Drive New Jersey Insurance Company erroneously sued as Progressive Casualty Insurance Company"). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
